---
title: "11 — Statistics (t-test, chi-square, ANOVA)"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

## Descriptive statistics

```{r}
x <- c(55, 62, 48, 71, 59, 44, 67, 53)
mean(x)
median(x)
sd(x)
var(x)
range(x)
diff(range(x)) # min, max, spread
quantile(x, probs = c(0.25, 0.75))
IQR(x)
summary(x)
```

## One-sample t-test

```{r}
# H0: population mean = 60
t.test(x, mu = 60)
```

## Two-sample independent t-test (Student)

```{r}
group_a <- c(55, 62, 48, 71, 59)
group_b <- c(44, 67, 53, 70, 41)
t.test(group_a, group_b, var.equal = TRUE) # Student (equal variance)
t.test(group_a, group_b) # Welch (unequal variance, default)
```

## Paired t-test

```{r}
before <- c(120, 135, 118, 141, 127)
after <- c(115, 128, 112, 135, 119)
t.test(before, after, paired = TRUE)
# Equivalent:
t.test(before - after, mu = 0)
```

## Reading p-value

```{r}
res <- t.test(group_a, group_b)
res$p.value # p-value
res$statistic # t statistic
res$conf.int # 95% confidence interval
```

A p-value < 0.05 → reject H0 at 5% significance level.

## Chi-square test (independence)

```{r}
# Two categorical variables
tab <- table(mtcars$cyl, mtcars$am)
tab
chisq.test(tab)
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
# Or from a cross-tabulation
chisq.test(matrix(c(10, 20, 30, 40), nrow = 2))
```

## ANOVA (one-way)

```{r}
# H0: means are equal across groups
res_aov <- aov(mpg ~ factor(cyl), data = mtcars)
summary(res_aov)
```

```{r}
# Post-hoc: which groups differ?
TukeyHSD(res_aov)
```

## Correlation

```{r}
cor(mtcars$wt, mtcars$mpg) # Pearson (default)
cor.test(mtcars$wt, mtcars$mpg) # with p-value
cor(mtcars[, c("mpg", "wt", "hp")]) # correlation matrix
```

## Normal distribution checks

```{r fig.height=4}
x <- rnorm(100, mean = 50, sd = 10)
shapiro.test(x) # H0: data is normally distributed
qqnorm(x)
qqline(x) # Q-Q plot: points on line = normal
```

## tapply — group statistics (base R)

```{r}
tapply(mtcars$mpg, mtcars$cyl, mean) # mean mpg per cyl group
tapply(mtcars$mpg, mtcars$cyl, sd)
```
